evals.name: initial-github-sync-setup
evals.prompt: I just started using SpecWeave on my project at github.com/myorg/backend-api. I want to sync my specs to GitHub Projects so my team can track progress. How do I set up the GitHub integration? I need two-way sync.
evals.expected_output: Should guide through GitHub CLI authentication (gh auth login), explain the config.json setup for specweave-github plugin with repo, autoSyncSpecs, syncDirection settings, explain that specs (not increments) are synced to GitHub Projects, and mention the sw-github:sync-spec command for manual sync.

assertions.text: Mentions GitHub CLI (gh) installation and authentication steps (gh auth login or gh auth status)
Shows or references the .specweave/config.json configuration with specweave-github plugin settings including repo, autoSyncSpecs, and syncDirection fields
Clarifies that SpecWeave syncs specs (living docs) to GitHub, NOT increments, and explains why (specs are permanent, increments are temporary)
Explains two-way sync capability and mentions syncDirection: two-way as the default configuration
References the sw-github:sync-spec command for triggering manual sync operations

evals.name: sync-troubleshooting-permissions
evals.prompt: My GitHub sync keeps failing with a 403 error when I run sw-github:sync-spec spec-003. I'm getting 'insufficient permissions' errors. I set up the token but something is wrong. How do I fix this?
evals.expected_output: Should diagnose GitHub permissions issues: check gh auth status, verify token scopes (needs repo and project access), check repository write access, suggest re-authenticating with correct scopes, and mention rate limit checking with gh api rate_limit.

assertions.text: Suggests running gh auth status to verify current authentication state and token validity
Addresses token scope/permissions requirements - mentions needing repo write access or project access scopes
Suggests re-authenticating (gh auth login) with correct scopes as a resolution step
Mentions checking GitHub API rate limits (gh api rate_limit) as a potential cause of failures
Suggests verifying the .specweave/config.json sync configuration and repo field correctness

evals.name: understand-sync-mapping
evals.prompt: Can you explain how SpecWeave maps to GitHub? I want to understand what becomes what. We have spec-001 with 5 user stories and each story has 3-4 acceptance criteria. What will this look like in GitHub?
evals.expected_output: Should explain the mapping: Spec -> GitHub Project (with title, description, columns), User Stories -> GitHub Issues (with AC as checkboxes, labels), progress tracking via checkbox updates, and automatic project column movement (Backlog -> In Progress -> Done).

assertions.text: Explains that a spec maps to a GitHub Project with title format [SPEC-001], description, and columns (Backlog, In Progress, Done)
Explains that each user story maps to a GitHub Issue with title format [US-001] and acceptance criteria as checkboxes in the issue body
Describes acceptance criteria being represented as checkboxes in GitHub Issues that get checked/unchecked as ACs are completed
Mentions GitHub labels being applied to issues (e.g., user-story, spec:spec-001, priority labels)
Explains automatic progress tracking - project board column movement or percentage updates as tasks/ACs are completed

evals.name: projects-v2-configuration
evals.prompt: We're using GitHub Projects V2 (the new board view). I want to set up SpecWeave to use our existing Projects V2 board (project number 5) with custom status fields. Our board has columns: Triage, Ready, Building, Review, Shipped. How do I configure this?
evals.expected_output: Should explain Projects V2 configuration with projectV2Enabled: true and projectV2Number: 5, custom status field mappings from SpecWeave states (planned, in-progress, completed) to the user's custom column names, and priority field mappings.

assertions.text: Shows or references the projectV2Enabled: true and projectV2Number: 5 configuration settings in the sync config
Explains statusFieldMapping configuration that maps SpecWeave states (planned, in-progress, completed) to custom column names
Mentions or shows priorityFieldMapping configuration for mapping P1-P4 priorities to custom priority field values
Addresses using an existing Projects V2 board by referencing its project number rather than creating a new one
